hypothesis: SESSION-LOG +?P entries cause eval_sufficiency._load_session_log() regex to silently drop sessions. Fixing will reveal true avg_lp is significantly higher than reported 2.0.

method.fix_1: sync_state._update_session_log() now parses PRINCIPLES.md 'Last batch scan' header to extract per-session principle counts. Replaces +?P with +NP for all existing entries.
method.fix_2: eval_sufficiency._load_session_log() regex updated to match +?P as +0P (graceful degradation for any remaining unresolved entries).
method.fix_3: _fix_stale_principle_counts() function added — in-place repair of existing SESSION-LOG entries using known principle counts.

root_cause: sync_state._update_session_log() wrote +?P for all backfilled sessions because it had no principle extraction logic. eval_sufficiency regex r'S(\d+).*?\+(\d+)L.*?\+(\d+)P' requires \d+ for principles — +?P fails to match, silently dropping the session. Result: 80% of sessions invisible to eval metrics.
mechanism: L3 strategic: data pipeline staleness silently invalidates metrics. The eval tool was measuring correctly — its input data was corrupted. This is a sensor failure (P-244), not an algorithm failure. The swarm was performing at EXCELLENT level for many sessions without knowing it.
implication: When a metric shows threshold-floor readings (exactly 2.0), check if the data pipeline is complete before recalibrating the threshold. Threshold artifacts (L-919) can come from data staleness, not measurement design.
